character_fourier_matrix.meaning: canonical 39x39 pivot submatrix of the irreducible A985 character table; useful as a Fourier/eigenvalue table, but not a modular S-matrix.

modular_boundary.extra_input_needed: a certified braiding or Hopf-link pairing on the 39 sectors, equivalently a ribbon element whose action is not just the transpose/dual identity.
modular_boundary.not_a_failure_of_linear_algebra: The Drinfeld/Grothendieck center and full A985 character table remain certified; this stage identifies the missing categorical input needed for modular data.
modular_boundary.reason: The certified data contains the semisimple A985 center, character table, tube-pair section, and duality. It does not contain a nontrivial braiding/Hopf-link operator. The natural annular swap candidates fail to preserve the 39-dimensional half-braiding subspace. The only stable canonical duality/ribbon candidate acts as identity on every primitive sector, giving T=I_39 and no nondegenerate modular S certificate.

verified_claims: The closed-loop transpose and tube dual-swap preserve HB_39 and act as identity on all 39 primitive Drinfeld idempotents.
Therefore the only canonical twist/ribbon operator present in the certified external_evidence_gate data gives T=I_39.
The annular pair-swap and dual-each pair-rotation do not preserve HB_39, so they cannot be used as modular ribbon/twist operators.
A deterministic 39x39 character-Fourier pivot matrix is invertible over F_1000003, but it is a character table slice, not modular S.
No nontrivial modular S,T pair is certified from the current data.
